key_points: Suite-style dorms leave students more isolated than community halls
Freshman dorm rooms described as extremely small and cramped
Students strongly recommend community-style dorms for social life
key_quotes: I'm in a dorm and I went with a suite style and I hate it. I wish I had done community so bad.
All of my friends in community dorms have become best friends with all of their hallmates.
The rooms are the size of matchboxes, two beds crammed into a single small living space.

key_points: Dorm choice heavily determines your social experience at K-State
Strong school spirit; friends and community form quickly for many students
Manhattan's isolation means social life is largely campus-dependent
key_quotes: I love K-State, they treat the students here like they really matter and honestly the atmosphere on campus during classes is great.
All of my friends in community dorms have become best friends with all of their hallmates.
Around the city is nothing.

key_points: In-state tuition around $15k/yr makes K-State a strong value option
International students face $45-50k/yr total cost with limited aid clarity
National Merit Finalists can attend for nearly free, a major draw

I'd be able to go to OU for basically free and probably the same for k-state.
Would cost about $60k total for in-state over four years.

Engineering and animal science programs mentioned positively multiple times
Ranked outside top tier for CS nationally, but considered solid for cost
